channel.title: SEA Cooperative & Adoption Tracker
channel.description: Ground-level cooperative activity and business practice change across Southeast Asian food systems — smallholder organising, sourcing commitments, and certification uptake.

item.title: An Giang rice cooperative opens shared drying and storage facility for 140 families
item.description: A rice cooperative federation in An Giang province has opened a shared drying and storage facility serving 140 smallholder families, unlocking direct-export contracts that previously required minimum volumes unreachable by individual farms. Organisers describe the facility as an agroecological transition asset, not merely an efficiency upgrade, because it removes a structural barrier to smallholder-centred marketing.

item.title: Sumatra coffee cooperative achieves bird-friendly certification across 2,600 hectares
item.description: A 700-member Arabica coffee cooperative in Aceh has completed bird-friendly certification covering 2,600 hectares of shade-grown plots. The cooperative says certification was a practical milestone rather than a goal in itself — part of a longer agroecological programme linking forest cover to member livelihoods.

item.title: Nestle SEA begins regenerative dairy sourcing pilot with Malaysian cooperatives
item.description: Nestle Southeast Asia has begun a regenerative dairy sourcing pilot in Malaysia covering roughly 380 smallholder dairy farms organised under two cooperative structures. The pilot pays a premium tied to verified soil-health and pasture-rotation metrics, with third-party measurement running over 24 months.

item.title: Thai organic rice farmer union secures direct-export licence after three-year campaign
item.description: A coalition of organic rice smallholders in Yasothon has been granted a direct export licence, ending a three-year campaign to bypass the country's traditional milling-exporter intermediaries. The union has pre-contracted its first container-load shipments to specialty distributors in Germany and Singapore, with proceeds distributed back to member households on a transparent formula.

item.title: Unilever SEA raises cooperative-sourced spice share to 28 per cent
item.description: Unilever's Southeast Asia business has reported that 28 per cent of its regional spice volumes — pepper, nutmeg, cinnamon — now originate from registered farmer cooperatives, up from 17 per cent two years ago. The company does not use the term agroecology, but the underlying procurement shift has materially changed who captures value in the supply chain.

item.title: Negros sugar cooperative adopts biochar and green-manure soil programme
item.description: A sugar cooperative on Negros island has adopted a biochar and green-manure programme across roughly 1,400 hectares, funded by a mix of member contributions and a regional agroecology grant. The shift is being framed by leadership as a practical step in a longer smallholder-centred transition away from industrial cane cultivation.

item.title: Bolaven coffee cooperative reaches 1,100 members after three mergers
item.description: A coffee cooperative on the Bolaven Plateau in Laos has reached 1,100 members following three consecutive village-level mergers over the past year. Leadership argues that consolidated governance gives smallholders the legal standing to engage development finance directly, rather than through an off-taker intermediary.

item.title: Olam signs five-year pricing floor with Indonesian cacao cooperative in Sulawesi
item.description: Olam Group has signed a five-year pricing-floor agreement with a 2,300-member cacao cooperative in South Sulawesi. The contract guarantees a minimum price independent of commodity market movements and commits the cooperative to agroforestry practices — an arrangement intermediate between conventional off-take and fully smallholder-centred direct trade.

item.title: Singapore commodity trader publishes agroecology-aligned procurement code
item.description: A mid-sized Singapore-headquartered commodity trader has published a procurement code explicitly aligned with agroecological principles, covering rice, pulses, and oilseeds across its SEA supply sheds. The code commits to cooperative-first sourcing where available and to a phased reduction in volume from monoculture estates over the next five years.

item.title: Myanmar mungbean cooperative achieves EU traceability compliance
item.description: A mungbean cooperative in the Sagaing region has completed EU deforestation-regulation traceability compliance covering 4,700 farmer plots. The certification unlocks direct European buyer access, though cooperative leadership cautions that compliance alone does not substitute for deeper work on soil and water.

item.title: Battambang rice cooperative commissions solar-powered irrigation network
item.description: A Cambodian rice cooperative in Battambang has commissioned a solar-powered irrigation network covering 480 hectares of member plots. The network was financed through a mix of member savings and a small concessional loan from a regional rural development fund, keeping ownership and maintenance inside the cooperative structure.

item.title: Vietnamese coastal fisheries cooperative secures group MSC certification
item.description: A coastal fisheries cooperative in Binh Dinh province has secured group Marine Stewardship Council certification covering 210 small-scale fishing vessels. The group-certification structure is a recent option designed for smallholder-scale operations that cannot afford individual audits.

item.title: Indonesian dairy cooperative overhauls governance to strengthen member voice
item.description: A 4,100-member dairy cooperative in East Java has completed a governance overhaul giving member-producers a majority on the board and introducing rotating representation from women dairy farmers. The restructuring is being watched by other regional cooperatives as a template for smallholder-equity reforms.

item.title: Thai organic vegetable cooperative enters Bangkok supermarket aisles
item.description: An organic vegetable cooperative in Chiang Mai has secured weekly supply contracts with two Bangkok supermarket chains, scaling beyond farmers' markets for the first time. The cooperative's 90 member farms continue to operate under agroecological management practices, which the supermarkets have agreed to reflect in shelf labelling.

item.title: Mindanao banana cooperative rejects plantation-expansion offer from multinational
item.description: A banana-growing cooperative in Bukidnon has publicly rejected an offer from a multinational fruit exporter to convert 600 hectares of diversified smallholder land into a single-variety plantation. Cooperative leadership cited concerns about transition lock-in and long-term soil degradation, opting instead to continue with a mixed-crop model.
